title: [proof-gap] sl_2 / 'bar G(A)=E(A) T(A) in all ranks' bridge in thm:looptorgen is asserted, not proved

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Type:** unjustified load-bearing claim hidden behind a citation (residual of prior Gap 2).

**Location:** `thm:looptorgen` "status" paragraph and `lem:steinberg` (loop case, identification G^circ = Inn(L)).

**The claim.** To deduce that inner gauge loops equal Inn(L), the proof reduces to a generation theorem and asserts the bridging statement:
> "for g=sl_2 the apparent failure 'E_2 != SL_2 over k[t^{+-1}]' is repaired exactly by adjoining the torus bar T(A) supplied by Lemma `lem:steinberg` and by passing to the adjoint group, so that bar G(A)=E(A).bar T(A) holds in all ranks."

**Why this is a gap.** By Cohn's theorem SL_2(k[t,t^{-1}]) is NOT generated by elementary matrices; SL_2/E_2 over a Laurent ring is a genuinely nontrivial quotient. The assertion that adjoining the diagonal torus T(A) (which lies in E(A) by the Steinberg relations!) "repairs" this and yields bar G(A)=E(A).bar T(A) at the adjoint level is exactly the delicate point and is presented as self-evident ("repaired exactly by"). Note: since h_alpha(u) in E(A) by Lemma steinberg, writing bar G(A)=E(A).bar T(A) with bar T(A) subset E(A) just says bar G(A)=E(A), i.e. the adjoint loop group is elementary-generated — which is precisely the strong statement that must be proved, not assumed.

**Required fix.** Replace the hand-wave with one of:
1. A precise citation of the Peterson-Kac generation theorem (PNAS 1983) for the affine Kac-Moody / loop group by *real root subgroups*, stated exactly for affine type A_1^{(1)}, with the descent from the minimal Kac-Moody group to L=L(g,sigma) spelled out and all hypotheses verified; OR
2. A direct rank-1 proof: using the homogeneous sl_2-triples (e t^i, f t^{-i}, h) and the Weyl/torus elements from Steinberg, prove that every sigma-equivariant Laurent loop into the adjoint group is a finite product of equivariant root-subgroup exponentials. 
Avoid the blanket "bar G(A)=E(A).bar T(A) in all ranks," which as written is either trivial-but-useless (T subset E) or false (E ⊊ SL_2).
